ledeShortJa: 細倉真弓（1979年京都生まれ）は、ヌード、身体、植物、鉱物、デジタルコラージュを通じて、身体を見る視線そのものを問う写真家。写真集《NEW SKIN》では男性身体、ゲイ雑誌、彫刻、セルフィーを重ね、男性身体を見る視線の位置を組み替えた。
ledeShortEn: Mayumi Hosokura (b. 1979, Kyoto) works with nudes, the body, plants, minerals, and digital collage to question the gaze that looks at the body. In her photobook NEW SKIN she layered the male body, gay magazines, sculpture, and selfies to rework the position of the gaze on the male body.

movementSlugJa: コンセプチュアルアート
starTextJa: 細倉真弓（1979年京都生まれ）は、2010年代以後の日本写真において、身体を見る視線の問題を扱う作家。写真集《Transparency is the new mystery》ではヌードと鉱物を透過的な白黒像として構成し、《NEW SKIN》では男性身体・ゲイ雑誌・彫刻・セルフィーをデジタルコラージュで重ね、身体と視線の境界を問い直した*1*2。
starTextEn: Mayumi Hosokura (b. 1979, Kyoto) addresses the gaze on the body in Japanese photography since the 2010s. In Transparency is the new mystery she composed nudes and minerals as translucent black-and-white images, and in NEW SKIN she layered the male body, gay magazines, sculpture, and selfies through digital collage to reconsider the boundary of body and gaze*1*2.
